scratchpad: [Compilation Check]: The code does not compile due to a syntax error in the if statement. | [Rubric Check]: The function does not handle case insensitivity or ignore spaces. | [Edge Cases]: The function will not work correctly due to syntax errors. | [Conclusion]: The function has both syntax and logical errors.

syntax_errors.issue: Single equals sign (=) used for comparison instead of double equals (==) in the if statement.
syntax_errors.fix: Change `if text = reversed_text:` to `if text == reversed_text:`

syntax_errors.issue: Missing return value in the else clause.

logical_errors.issue: The function does not ignore spaces as required by the rubric.
logical_errors.fix: Use the `.replace(" ", "")` method on the string before comparing it to its reversed version.

logical_errors.issue: The function is not case-insensitive as required by the rubric.
logical_errors.fix: Use the `.lower()` method on the string before comparing it to its reversed version.

overview: The student's code demonstrates a basic understanding of string reversal but lacks attention to syntax and logical handling of case and spaces.
